pi_items.verified_value: SPY is the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, the original and largest S&P 500 ETF
NinjaTrader 8 supports Day (D), Week (W), and Month (MO) timeframes as standard chart configurations
NinjaTrader 8 has built-in support for RSI (Relative Strength Index), MACD (Moving Average Convergence/Divergence), and SMA (Simple Moving Average) indicators
The 14-period RSI is the standard default period, as recommended by RSI developer J. Welles Wilder in his original work
MACD standard parameters are 12 (fast EMA), 26 (slow EMA), and 9 (signal line EMA). These are the default settings in virtually all charting software
10-period SMA is a valid configuration. While common standards are 10, 20, 50, 100, and 200, the 10-period SMA is used for short-term trend analysis and swing trading

pi_items.metadata_value: Real daily OHLCV data for SPY from January 2023 to December 2024
pi_items.verified_value: SPY historical OHLCV data for January 2023 - December 2024 is publicly available from multiple sources. SPY returned 26.19% in 2023 and 24.89% in 2024

pi_items.verified_value: NinjaTrader 8 includes a Workspaces menu and save functionality accessible via Workspaces menu or File > Save Workspace. Workspaces are saved to disk and can be loaded later

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a multi-timeframe trading workspace for SPY (State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust). SPY is a real, widely-traded equity instrument. The three required timeframes (Day, Week, Month) are standard configurations supported by NinjaTrader 8. The technical indicators (RSI with period 14, MACD with parameters 12-26-9, and SMA with period 10) use either industry-standard or commonly-used parameter values. Historical OHLCV data for SPY covering January 2023 to December 2024 is publicly available and real, with SPY showing returns of 26.19% in 2023 and 24.89% in 2024. NinjaTrader 8 supports workspace save functionality to persist configurations to disk. The task does not involve synthetic data.
